behavior_summary: Provides the 'U8P_MACE::look' (examine) handler for the mace item. When an actor/process examines the mace, the routine marks item info and sets a process-exclude flag, builds the literal label "mace ", calls Item::bark to present that label to the examining process, then frees the temporary string and suspends/returns.

The routine's primary visible effect is to emit the short text 'mace ' to the caller/process that triggered the U8P_MACE::look. The info and process-exclude state writes appear intended to control visibility/context of that message or the item's processing while the message is emitted.
Return value from Item::bark is captured and passed through an implies/pop sequence before the function suspends; the exact control effect of that value is inferred but not explicit in the snippet.
notable_control_flow: Sequence: write item info flag -> write process-exclude flag -> allocate/prepare literal string "mace " -> push caller/process pointer (BP+06h) and string -> call Item::bark -> free string -> capture retval -> implies/pop on retval -> store temporary -> suspend/return.
The handler is invoked as an event-driven process (a short-lived process execution context that performs state updates and UI output then suspends).
open_questions: What exact info key (numeric id) is being set and what semantic meaning does it carry for this item?
How precisely does process-exclude affect recipients or processing of the item (scope/temporal effect)?
What does the captured return value from Item::bark represent and how should the implies/pop result be interpreted in gameplay terms?
Is BP+06h always the player/avatar process that should receive the bark, or can other processes invoke this U8P_MACE::look handler?
Are there any side effects on global quest/interaction flags (none observed in this snippet, but full script context may change that)?

main_function.name: U8P_MACE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the examine/U8P_MACE::look event for the mace item: set item info and a process-exclude flag, then display the short label 'mace ' to the process that triggered the U8P_MACE::look (via Item::bark), and then suspend/return.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_MACE::look) — an actor/process examines or inspects the mace, triggering this handler.

function_map.name: U8P_MACE::look
function_map.role: Event handler for the mace item's 'U8P_MACE::look' (examine) event.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item-provided routine used to display or emit a short text/sound message tied to an item (here used to present 'mace ' to a target process).

external_calls.purpose: Emit/display the short label/message 'mace ' to the process that examined the item (BP+06h).

writes.name: info
writes.likely_effect: Set an item info/state key on the mace before emitting the U8P_MACE::look text (likely marks that info was shown or modifies item metadata/state).

writes.name: process_exclude
writes.likely_effect: Exclude the item or its process from normal processing during this operation, likely to control who receives the bark or to prevent re-entrancy while showing the label.
